messages.content:
South Africa ratified the Outer Space Treaty, an international agreement with over 100 signing
nations that acts as the foundation for the laws of space, on March 1, 1967. Colombia signed the
treaty;____it has yet to officially ratify it.
a) A. specifically,
c) C. however,
b) B. similarly,
d) D. for example
C. however,

Insects are the most numerous animals on earth, yet we think of them as living exclusively on land.
Surprisingly, there are five insect species of the genus Halobates that live their entire lives on the
ocean surface. Insects in the genus Halobates _______ a waxy substance that they spread over
their bodies, allowing them to sit on the ocean’s surface without getting wet.
Which choice completes the text with the most logical and precise word or phrase?
a) A. radiate
c) C. alleviate
b) B. secrete
d) D. exalt
b) B. secrete

The emphasis on accurately representing the experiences of average working people that is
characteristic of the realist style can be seen in The Gleaners, painted by jean-François Millet,
which depicts peasants picking stray wheat from a field after them harvest. This style can thus be
seen as an effort to what were regarded as the _______ excesses of the romantic style evident in
many paintings by Horace Veraet, which instead exaggerated their subject’s' beauty or heroism
while hiding, all imperfection.
Which choice completes the text with the most logical and precise word or phrase?
a) A. understand
c) C. counteract
b) B. advance
d) D. accentuate
c) C. counteract

The following text is from Charles Chesnutt’s 1905 novel The Colonel’s Dream. Mr. French
and Mr. Kirby work together.
Mr. French, the senior partner, who sat opposite Kirby, was an older man-a safe guess would have
placed him somewhere in the debatable ground between forty and fifty; of a good height, as could
be seen even from the seated figure, the upper part of which was held erect with the unconscious
ease which one associates with military training.
As used in the text, what does the word “placed” most nearly mean?
a) A. Arranged
c) C. Modified
b) B. Contracted
d) D. Estimated
d) D. Estimated

The following text is adapted from Anton Chekhov's 1904 play The Cherry Orchard
(translated by Julius West in 1916).
TROFIMOV: Believe me, Anya, believe me! I'm not thirty yet, I'm young. I'm still a student, but I
have undergone a great deal! I'm as hungry as the winter, I'm ill, I'm shaken...and where haven't I
been fate has tossed me everywhere!
As used in the text, what does the word "undergone" most nearly mean?
a) A. Neglected
c) C. Conveyed
b) B. Enjoyed
d) D. Endured
d) D. Endured

Brtish painter Peter Edward is known for his portraits of notable figures in different fields, from poet
Heaney to casting director Ruth Lambert. These works are widely praised, meaning that Edwards
receives substantial ________ as an artist.
Which choice completes the text with the most logical and precise word or phrase?
a) A acclaim
c) C sympathy
b) B tolerance
d) D guidance
a) A acclaim

The swordfish can swim very fast up to 97 kilometres per hour (km/hr)--but it is significantly slower
than the frigatebird, which can fly at speeds up to 153 km/hr .The difference between these speeds
is largely _______ of the fact that the features that make flight possible do less to limit top speeds
than the features suitable for swimming through water.
Which choice completes the text with the most logical and precise word or phrase?
a) A. a consequence
c) C. a repudiation
b) B. an explanation
d) D. an objective
a) A. a consequence

New and interesting research conducted by Suleiman A. Al-Sweedan and Moath Alhaj is inspired
by their observation that though studies of the effect of high altitude on blood chemistry are _____
the effect on blood chemistry of living in locations below sea level, such as the California towns of
Salton City and Coachella, has received comparatively little notice.
Which choice completes the text with the most logical and precise word or phrase?
a) A. preliminary
c) C. sporadic
b) B. equivocal
d) D. abundant
d) D. abundant

The Far Side of the World, first published in 1984, is a novel in Patrick O Brians Aubrey/Maturin
series, which includes twenty completed books. Some critics have found fault with the abrupt
endings of The Far Side of the World and other books in the series, saying that they do not finish
conclusively but arbitrarily stop. But other critics argue that the books should not be thought of as
discrete texts with traditional beginnings and endings but as a single incredibly long work, similar to
other multivolume stories, such as Marcel Proust's In Search of Lost Time.
Which choice best states the main purpose of the text?
a) A. To present a reason the unusual structure
that O'Brian uses for The Far Side of the
World makes it one of his most complex
books
c) C. To describe a characteristic of the
Aubrey/Maturin novels and offer two differing
viewpoints on this characteristic
b) B. To explain why many critics find the
Aubrey/Maturin novels to be remarkably
entertaining despite flaws in the novels'
structures
d) D. To argue that the Aubrey/Maturin series
should have the literary renown of In Search
of Lost Time
c) C. To describe a characteristic of the Aubrey/Maturin novels and offer two differing viewpoints on this characteristic

Researchers César A. Hidalgo, Elisa Castaner, and Andres Sevtsuk created a computer model to
predict the mix of electronics stores, bakeries, and other businesses found in a given
neighborhood. How we define a neighborhood and its boundaries is subjective, so the team used a
clustering algorithm to locate dense groupings of amenities that represent human-identified
neighborhoods like Boston's Central Square. The predictive model, which incorporates this
algorithm, is sure to be invaluable in determining the optimal mix of city’s amenities.
Which choice best describes the function of the underlined portion in the text as a whole?
a) A. It emphasizes the accuracy of an
algorithm used to identify the boundaries of
specific neighborhoods.
c) C. It criticizes the methods that urban
planners traditionally use to define
neighborhoods.
b) B. It suggests a difficulty associated with
analyzing neighborhoods that the research
team attempted to overcome.
d) D. It introduces a study about the clustering
of amenities in urban neighborhoods.
b) B. It suggests a difficulty associated with analyzing neighborhoods that the research team attempted to overcome.

The following text is adapted from Lady Gregory's 1904 play Spreading the News. Jack
Smith and Bartley Fallon have encountered each other at the local fair.
JACK: It isn't to the fair I came myself, but up to the Five Acre Mesdow I'm going, where I have a
contract for the hay. We'll get a share of it into tramps (drying stacks)to-day.
BARTLEY: You will not get it into tramps to-day. The rain will be down on it by evening and on
myself too. It's seldom I ever started on a journey but the rain would come down on me before I'd
find any place of shelter.
JACK: If it didn't itself, Bartley, it is my belief you would carry a leaky pail on your head in place of a
hat, that way you'd not be without some cause of complaining.
Based on the text, what does jack most likely believe about Bartley?
a) A. Bartley and Jack will be better able to cut
the hay if they work together.
c) C. Bartley's forecast of rain is mistaken.
b) B. Bartley is concerned that jack hasn't
prepared for the weather.
d) D. Bartley is always unhappy about
something.
d) D. Bartley is always unhappy about something.

Horizontal gene transfer involves the exchange of genetic material between organisms not in a
parent-offspring relationship. It is common among prokaryotes (single celled organisms such as the
bacteria Brevundimonas diminuta and Hafinia alver) . The process can have the effect of increasing
bacterial resistance to antibiotics; as such, an understanding of how to prevent horizontal gene
transfer might result in the mitigation of dangerous pathogens (organisms that cause disease).
Which choice best states the main idea of the text?
a) A. A common perception of horizontal gene
transfer is inaccurate.
c) C. The study of horizontal gene transfer may
yield findings with useful applications.
b) B. Horizontal gene transfer is more common
than was initially thought.
d) D. The results of new research into horizontal
gene transfer cast doubt on earlier findings.
c) C. The study of horizontal gene transfer may yield findings with useful applications.

In a study of new technology adoption, Davit Marikyan et al. examined negative disconfirmation
(which occurs when experiences fall short of one’s expectations) to determine whether it could lead
to positive outcomes for users. The team focused on established users of “smart home” technology,
which presents inherent utilization challenges but tends to attract users with high expectations,
often leading to feelings of dissonance. The researchers found that many users employed cognitive
mechanisms to mitigate those feelings, ultimately reversing their initial sense of disappointment.
Which choice best states the main idea of the text?
a) A) Research suggests that most users of
smart home technology will not achieve a
feeling of satisfaction given the utilization
challenges of such technology.
c) C) Research suggests that users with high
expectations for a new technology can feel
content with that technology even after
experiencing negative disconfirmation.
b) B) Although most smart home technology is
aimed at meeting or exceeding users’ high
expectations, those expectations in general
remain poorly understood.
d) D) Although negative discon¦rmation has
often been studied, little is known about the
cognitive mechanisms shaping users’
reactions to it in the context of new
technology adoption.
c) C) Research suggests that users with high expectations for a new technology can feel content with that technology even after experiencing negative disconfirmation.

In order to identify research trends, Shirley Ann Williams et al, reviewed a collection of studies of
the social media website Twitter, such as the 2011 paper by Celik et al, titled "Learning Semantic
Relationships between Entities in Twitter." Williams's team searched for the term "Twitter" on the
Scopus and Web of Science databases and found that, though most papers returned by the search
did in fact focus on the social media platform, a few discussed unrelated subjects such as sounds
made by tractor engines. One reasonable explanation for this result is that it occurred because
_____
Which choice most logically completes the text?
a) A) a group of researchers had an extensive
discussion on Twitter about the design of
tractor engines.
c) C) the word "twitter" may not always refer to a
social media website, but to other subjects
such as noises made by machines.
b) B) most of the papers in the Scopus and Web
of Science databases do not discuss
automotive engines or social media websites.
d) D) academics who are active on social
networks are likely to announce their recent
publications on Twitter.
c) C) the word "twitter" may not always refer to a social media website, but to other subjects such as noises made by machines.

Arthurian legends (tales related to the character of King Arthur) derive from many sources, such as
Annales Cambriae, composed around 970, and Tom a Lincoln from around 1607. One of the most
significant sources, Geoffrey of Monmouths History of the Kings of Britain, was written in the 1130s;
some material from it was later adapted by the Norman poet Wace into the Roman de Brut in 1155.
But while the Roman de Brut includes references to the famous Round Table at which Arthur's
knights assembled, nothing written before 1155 does, which suggests that ______
Which choice most logically completes the test?
a) A. Geoffrey of Monouth was unaware of
stories of the Round Table when composing
his History, though historians know that works
containing such stories were available to him.
c) C. Roman de Brut is more historically
accurate than History, because Tom a Lincoln
had not been written when Geoffrey of
Monmouth was writing his work.
b) B. Geoffrey of Monmouth' accounts of
Arthurian legends in his History are more
similar overall in content to the accounts in
Tom a Lincoln than they are to the accounts
in Roman de Brut.
d) D. Any Arthurian legend that mentions the
Round Table likely has a lineage that
connects it to Roma
d) D. Any Arthurian legend that mentions the Round Table likely has a lineage that connects it to Roman de Brut or works that were directly or indirectly influenced by it.

In a 2012 study in the United States, Michael E. Berndt and Travis K. Bavin found a positive
association between levels of dissolved organic carbon and mercury in bodies of fresh water. Many
other studies have yielded similar results, suggesting to some scientists that this association is true
for all bodies of fresh water. But much of that research has been conducted at broadly similar sites
in North America, and when Seam Noh and colleagues examined bodies of fresh water in South
Korea, they found a negative association between dissolved organic carbon levels and mercury
levels. If similar findings emerge from other locations outside North America, that could suggest
that _____
Which choice most logically completes the text?
a) A) the mercury levels reported in Noh and
colleagues' study were much higher than
those reported in the study by Brebdt and
Bavin even though the dissolved organic
carbon levels reported in the two studies
were approximately the same.
c) C) the relationship between dissolved organic
carbon and mercury reported by Berndt and
Bavin reflects conditions that are
characteristic of certain kinds of ecosystems
in North America rather than universal
conditions.
b) B) Berndt and Bavin may have inadvertently
measured a different characteristic of bodies
of fresh water than their levels of dissolved
organic carbon and mercury.
d) D) most of the studies conducted in North
America have measured dissolved organic
carbon and mercury levels at a higher level of
precision than was the case in Noh and
colleagues' study.

c) C) the relationship between dissolved organic carbon and mercury reported by Berndt and Bavin reflects conditions that are characteristic of certain kinds of ecosystems in North America rather than universal conditions.

Drosophila (fruit flies) have generation times of 10-12 days, so seasonal changes in food
availability and other environmental conditions can drive seasonal fluctuations in chromosome
rearrangements in species such as D.robusta and D.subobscura. Drosophila body size (for which
wing centroid size serves as a proxy measure) correlates with life span. Banu Sebum Onder and
Gansu Fidan Akosy measured the wing size of members of D. melangaster population in Yegiloz,
Turkey, that were collected monthly between May and October over three years. Their research
suggests that Drosophila collected in relatively cooler months should tend to have a shorter life
span, as illustrated by the finding that _______
Which choice completes the sentence with accurate and relevant information from the
table?
a) A. the average male wing centroid size was
consistently smaller than the average female
wing centroid size in all four months in the
table.
c) C. the average male wing centroid size was
smaller in May than in July.
b) B. the average female wing centroid size was
2.02 mm in July but was 2.31 mm in June.
d) D. the average monthly low temperature was
lower in May than in June

I'm sorry, but it seems there might have been an oversight, as there is no actual data or a table provided in your question to refer to for completing the sentence accurately. Without specific data from a table, it's not possible to accurately complete the sentence with information such as wing centroid sizes for Drosophila in particular months or temperature details.

Could you provide the table or the data needed to accurately complete the sentence?
no there is no graph

Without specific data or a graph to refer to, I can't provide an answer that directly corresponds to specific findings. However, I can suggest a logical completion based on typical relations between temperature, life span, and wing size in Drosophila, mentioned in the premise of your question. 

Given the context you provided—that Drosophila collected in cooler months tend to have a shorter lifespan—and relating this to the components of the question, a logical completion may involve discussing findings that illustrate the connection between cooler temperatures and aspects of Drosophila physiology or life history traits that could correlate to lifespan, such as body or wing size. Since the actual data or findings were to be illustrated by the sentence completion, in a general context, based on known principles:

If the research suggests that Drosophila collected in relatively cooler months should tend to have a shorter life span, a logical yet hypothetical completion could be along the lines of a generic pattern observed in biological studies, such as:

"...as illustrated by the finding that Drosophila collected during the cooler months, like May and October, had smaller wing sizes compared to those collected in warmer months, reflecting a potential physiological adaptation that correlates with the expected shorter lifespan."

Remember, this completion is fabricated, aligning with common biological outcomes, and based on the premise that cooler conditions might lead to observable changes in Drosophila that relate to life history traits like lifespan. Without specific data, this completion is speculative and aimed to fit within a logical expectation based on your scenario.

A student is writing an essay on the subject of cultured meat, which is grown in a laboratory and is
intended to help reduce the number of livestock harvested for food. The student wishes to make
the case that people have mixed feelings about cultured meat that may be a barrier to its worldwide
adoption.
Which quotation from a publication by a researcher would most effectively support the
student's claim?
a) A The flavor of meat from livestock differs
across species (from pig to chicken to cow),
and is also influenced by farming conditions
and the breeds and genders of animals.
c) C Many consumers who were surveyed
believed that cultured meat would be good for
the environment if widely available, but few of
those same consumers were willing to try
eating cultured meat themselves.
b) B Advocates of lab grown meat claim that it's
better for the environment than conventional
meat because it requires less land.
d) D Consumers tend to believe that using less
packaging when selling meat products in
stores would have a significant effect on the
environment.

c) Many consumers who were surveyed believed that cultured meat would be good for the environment if widely available, but few of those same consumers were willing to try eating cultured meat themselves.

This quotation directly supports the student's claim by illustrating the mixed feelings people have about cultured meat—acknowledging its potential environmental benefits yet showing reluctance to consume it themselves.

An emulsifier is a type of compound that serves to stabilize an emulsion-a mixture of two or more
liquids that otherwise would not easily blend together, In the cosmetics industry, emulsifiers such as
polysorbate 80______to blend oil and water into homogeneous formulations, like lotions and
perfumes.
Which choice completes the text so that it conforms to the conventions of Standard English?
a) A. being employed
c) C. are employed
b) B. that they employ
d) D. employed
c) C. are employed

While researching a topic, a student has taken the following notes:
- Morning Haiku (2010) is a book of postry by African American poet Sonia Sanchez,
- Each poem in the book is a sequence of haiku.
- According to the books publisher, Penguin Random House, the book "celebrates the gifts of life and
mourns the deaths of revered African American figures."
- The poem “7 haiku (for Ray Brown)” is written as a sequence of seven haiku.
- The poem "6 haiku (for Beauford Delaney) is written as a sequence of six haiku.
The student wants to contrast the number of haiku in each poem. Which choice most
effectively uses relevant information from the notes to accomplish this goal?
a) A. While “7 haiku (for Ray Brown)" is about
bassist Ray Brown, “6 haiku (for Beauford
Delaney)” is about painter Beauford Delaney.
c) C. The poem “7 haiku (for Ray
Brown)”consists of seven haiku, whereas the
poem "6 haiku (for Beauford
Delaney)”consists of six.
b) B. Both “7 haiku (for Ray Brown)” and “6
haiku (for Beauford Delaney)” can be found in
Sanchez's 2010 collection Morning Haiku.
d) D. The poems in Morning Haiku celebrate the
lives or mourn the deaths of revered African
American ¦gures," according to the book's
publisher, Penguin Random Hous
c) C. The poem “7 haiku (for Ray Brown)” consists of seven haiku, whereas the poem "6 haiku (for Beauford Delaney)” consists of six.
